title: Dimension Hierarchy
description: Describes the tree structure of a hierarchical dimension, such as administrative boundaries or coded indicator trees. The provider type determines how hierarchy operations (/children, /ancestors, ?parent=) are implemented — the hierarchy object is purely descriptive metadata for clients. 'levels' is present when the provider uses a leveled strategy (hierarchy imposed by named level definitions). 'parent_property' is present when the provider uses a recursive strategy (each member carries a parent reference). Both may be absent for custom providers that encode hierarchy differently. Navigation: when the provider declares navigable:true and a client passes ?links=true, each member in a /items or /children response MAY include per-member navigation links (rel:children for non-leaf members, rel:ancestors). By default, member-level links are suppressed to minimize response size; response-level pagination links (self, next, prev) are always included.

strategy.description: Informational annotation describing the hierarchy strategy. The generator type is the authoritative source of hierarchy behaviour — this field documents the strategy for human readers and clients that want to render appropriate UI. 'leveled': hierarchy is imposed by named level definitions; the tree structure is not encoded in member data. 'recursive': each member carries a parent reference field in its output. 'composite': leveled at the top levels, recursive within a level (future).

levels.description: Ordered list of hierarchy levels, from coarsest (index 0) to finest. Present when the provider uses a leveled strategy. Each level specifies the provider parameters that select its members, the output field that serves as the member ID, and the output field that carries the cross-level parent reference.

id.description: Unique identifier for this level within the hierarchy (e.g., 'L0', 'country', 'adm1').

label.description: Human-readable name for this level in the default language (e.g., 'Country', 'Region').

labels.description: Human-readable names for this level in multiple languages. Keys are RFC 5646 Language-Tags (e.g. 'en', 'fr', 'ar'); values are the level name in that language. Complements the single-language 'label' field: when 'labels' is present, clients SHOULD prefer labels[negotiated_lang] over 'label'. The 'label' field remains REQUIRED as the default-language fallback.

size.description: Number of distinct members at this level. If omitted, clients retrieve count from the provider's extent.

parent_level.description: The id of the parent level in this hierarchy. Absent for the root level.

member_id_property.description: The property in the provider's output object that uniquely identifies members at this level. Defaults to 'code' if omitted.

parent_id_property.description: The property in the provider's output object that holds the identifier of the parent member (from the parent level). Required when parent_level is set.

parameters.description: Provider parameters that, when passed to the /items endpoint, produce only members belonging to this level. The backend-agnostic generalization of a SQL condition or CQL filter.

href.description: Link to the paginated endpoint for members at this level. Parameters from the 'parameters' field SHOULD be encoded as query parameters on this URI.

parent_property.description: The property in the provider's output schema that holds the parent member's code. Null for root members. Present when the provider uses a recursive strategy. Corresponds to skos:broader in SKOS vocabularies.

root_filter.description: Optional provider parameter expression that selects root (top-level) members when passed to /items. Used with recursive providers. If omitted, root members are those whose parent_property is null or absent.

depth.description: Optional maximum depth of the tree. Informational; providers MAY enforce this limit.
